mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 132,414k suggests ongoing but moderating employment growth
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with sustained labor market expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, real rates turn negative, incentivizing equity allocation as a hedge against currency debasement and producing positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% and CPIAUCSL at 212.671 suggest near-zero nominal rates with positive inflation
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with persistent inflation expectations
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10) when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 1% signals weakening consumer demand, which compresses near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 337,677M reflects consumer spending levels
regime_hint: demand-sensitive market environment with high equity beta to consumption shocks
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances where RSAFS declines > 1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth acceleration above 0.3% month-over-month indicates strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains, which support profit margins and drive positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.0723 reflects current industrial activity levels
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with improving capacity utilization
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of months with INDPRO growth > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) combined with retail sales (RSAFS) both declining month-over-month signals synchronized weakness across residential and consumer demand channels, producing negative 20-day forward returns as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < -0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 510k and RSAFS at 337,677M reflect current demand conditions
regime_hint: demand-shock regime with synchronized weakness across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where both triggers are met, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.9% reflects current labor market slack
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, the Fed faces implicit pressure to tighten, creating uncertainty about future policy and producing negative 20-day forward returns as equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,340.9B and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% suggest policy accommodation amid growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with policy normalization expectations
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) growth above 15% year-over-year signals accelerating residential investment and forward demand, which supports construction employment and materials demand, producing positive 60-day forward returns through a multiplier effect on corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 510k reflects current housing activity levels
regime_hint: housing-led recovery with strong residential investment momentum
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances where HOUST YoY growth exceeds 15%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
